action: Review and approve the Q1 2026 board security brief before the March 15 board meeting. No board decisions required this quarter.
rationale: Brief synthesizes Q1 security posture improvement (+4 points to 67/100), 2 incidents both fully resolved, and 1 NIS2 compliance gap with remediation plan.
key_messages: Our security posture improved 6% this quarter — we are on track to reach the 75-point target by Q3.
We responded to 2 security incidents; neither resulted in data exposure. Average response time was 4 hours.
The EU NIS2 Directive requires 3 control updates by September 2026 — plan and budget are in place.

sections.posture_summary: Security posture score: 67/100 (Fair, up 4 points from Q4 2025). Identity & Access strongest domain at 80/100. Detection weakest domain at 58/100 — active improvement program underway.
sections.key_incidents: 2 incidents in Q1: (1) Phishing campaign targeting finance team — detected in 2 hours, no accounts compromised. (2) Third-party vendor data exposure — contained in 6 hours, affected customers notified.
sections.compliance_status: SOC 2 Type II: Green. PCI DSS v4.0: Green. GDPR: Green. NIS2 (EU): Yellow — 3 control gaps, remediation by September 2026.
sections.investments: Q1 security investments: $180K on endpoint detection expansion (coverage now 97%), $45K on security awareness training (phishing click rate down 40%).
sections.outlook: Q2 priorities: (1) Improve Detection domain to 65+, (2) Complete NIS2 gap remediation, (3) Launch AI red teaming program for customer-facing AI features.

key_findings: Posture score: 67/100 — up 4 points from Q4 2025
2 incidents resolved with no data exposure
NIS2 compliance gap: 3 controls need remediation by September 2026
Phishing click rate reduced 40% following awareness training
